penalties.description: The marked circle was not completely cut out by the end of the video sequence. The circular piece remains partially attached to the gauze.

consensus_score.reasoning: Both models detected cutting activity following the marked circle path. Claude identified incomplete task completion with the circular piece remaining partially attached, while GPT-4o assumed completion with a line deviation penalty. Given that both models noted the final state was not fully visible and Claude's observation of incomplete circle release aligns with the automatic-zero condition for incomplete tasks, the incomplete_circle penalty takes precedence.

claude_value.description: The marked circle was not completely cut out by the end of the video sequence. The circular piece remains partially attached to the gauze.

gpt_value.description: Cut deviated outside the marked circle during the procedure.

disagreements.rationale: Incomplete task completion is an automatic-zero condition that supersedes accuracy penalties. Both models noted limited visibility of the final state, but Claude's identification of incomplete circle release is consistent with the rubric requirement for complete circle cutout.
Claude provided more detailed reasoning about timer progression from 03:00 to approximately 01:04, supporting the 116-second timing.

disagreements.resolution: Used 0.0 score due to incomplete task
disagreements.rationale: Incomplete circle penalty results in automatic zero score per rubric, regardless of timing performance.
